UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile in the dating app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile.

location.description: User's current location (e.g., coordinates or address).

Match.title: Match
Match.description: Represents a match between two users.

user1Id.description: Reference to the first user in the match. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Match)

user2Id.description: Reference to the second user in the match. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Match)

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents a chat message between two users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the chat message.

senderId.description: Reference to the user who sent the message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatMessage)

receiverId.description: Reference to the user who received the message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatMessage)

matchId.description: Reference to the match associated with the message. (Relationship: Match 1:N ChatMessage)

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the message was sent.

Like.title: Like
Like.description: Represents a user's like or dislike of another user.

likerId.description: Reference to the user who is liking. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Like)

likeeId.description: Reference to the user who is being liked. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Like)

isLike.description: Indicates whether the user liked (true) or disliked (false) the other user.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the like/dislike was recorded.

LikedBy.title: LikedBy
LikedBy.description: Represents a record of a user being liked by another.

likerId.description: ID of the user who performed the like.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the like was recorded.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their own profile.
Stores the likes/dislikes that this user has sent to other users. The document ID can be the ID of the 'likee'.
Stores records of other users who have liked this user. The document ID can be the ID of the 'liker'.
Stores match data between two users. Includes denormalized 'user1Id' and 'user2Id' for authorization independence.
Stores chat messages for a specific match.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the core features of the GoldChic Dating app, focusing on user profiles, matching, likes, and real-time chat. The design emphasizes authorization independence and efficient data retrieval, adhering to the core design principles.

*   **UserProfile:** User profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}`. This path-based ownership ensures that only the user can read and write their profile data.
*   **Likes & LikedBy (Subcollections):** To manage relationships efficiently, two subcollections are used under each user's profile:
    *   `/users/{userId}/likes`: This stores all the 'like' or 'dislike' actions performed by the user. It makes it easy to query for all the people a user has swiped on.
    *   `/users/{userId}/likedBy`: This stores who has liked the current user. This allows the 'Who liked me' feature to be implemented with a simple collection query, which is highly efficient.
*   **Matches:** Matches are stored in a top-level `/matches/{matchId}` collection to keep the data structure flat. Each match document contains a `users` array with the UIDs of the two matched users. This design allows for efficient querying of all matches for a given user (e.g., `where('users', 'array-contains', currentUserId)`).
*   **Chat Messages:** Chat messages are a subcollection of a match (`/matches/{matchId}/messages/{messageId}`). This structure logically groups all messages for a conversation and allows security rules to easily grant access only to the participants of the parent match document.

This structure promotes security, scalability, and debuggability by minimizing hierarchical dependencies and clearly defining ownership and access rights.
